column5: 150-300 hours/year maintaining battlecards on 5 competitors with quarterly refresh cycle. Bouncing between G2, press releases, and LinkedIn stalking.

column5: Rep froze on a competitive call, $50K deal flatlined. The people making the battlecards aren't the ones using them.
column8: MiAmar bridges this gap — AI meeting prep generates a 60-second brief for any meeting type (19+ supported). Reps get the specific competitive context they need for THAT call, not a generic 10-page doc.

Reps need the right intel at the right moment, not a 10-page PDF they'll never open. AI meeting prep that generates a 60-second brief before each call is what actually moves the needle.

column5: Sales rep: It's six months out of date, the content is of no use to me. Brand approval processes take 6 weeks — card is stale before it ships.
column8: MiAmar battlecards update automatically with no approval bottleneck — they're generated from live data. The weekly newsletter provides a curated summary so the whole team stays current without formal review cycles.

column10: The 6-week approval cycle kills any CI program. By the time legal and brand sign off, the competitor has already shipped their next release. The answer is automated cards that update continuously from public data — no approval needed because the source material is all public.

column5: 58% of CI professionals say keeping battlecards and content updated is their biggest struggle
column8: MiAmar's core value prop: battlecards that never go stale. Daily monitoring of LinkedIn posts, profiles, news, funding, and job postings feeds into auto-generated cards with elevator pitch, why-we-win, objection handling, and recent moves.

column10: If 58% of CI pros struggle with freshness, the answer isn't try harder — it's automation. Self-updating battlecards that pull from daily monitoring data mean the freshness problem disappears entirely. The PMM's job shifts from data entry to strategy.

column5: 58% can't gather competitive intel in a timely manner — even with dedicated CI teams
column8: MiAmar monitors 24/7 automatically — LinkedIn company posts, profiles, news, press, funding rounds, and job postings. AI classifies events by type and extracts key takeaways. Data refreshes daily.

column10: Timeliness is the whole game in CI. A competitor funding announcement you learn about 2 weeks late is useless. Automated daily monitoring with AI classification turns reactive scrambling into proactive awareness. The data comes to you.

column5: Without tooling, PMMs spend 15-20 hrs/week on CI. 50-person sales org loses $400K+/year in labor to manual competitor research.

Automated CI tools pay for themselves in week one.

column5: Klue/Crayon cost $20K-$40K/year — completely inaccessible to startups, SMBs, and teams without dedicated CI budget
column8: MiAmar is purpose-built for PMMs and PMs at companies that can't justify $30K/year for Klue. Full CI platform — battlecards, event intelligence, prospect cards, integrations — at a fraction of the enterprise price.

column5: Google Alerts: inconsistent delivery, slow (24hr delay), noisy results, misses Reddit/social/podcasts entirely
column8: MiAmar replaces Google Alerts with purpose-built competitor monitoring. Tracks LinkedIn posts, profiles, news, funding, and job postings with AI classification — far more comprehensive and less noisy than Google Alerts.
column9: Monitoring of Reddit, podcasts, YouTube, and other social channels beyond LinkedIn (broader signal coverage for teams that care about community sentiment)
column10: Google Alerts was designed for general web mentions, not competitive intelligence. It misses social, has a 24-hour delay, and floods you with irrelevant results. Purpose-built CI tools monitor the specific channels that matter (LinkedIn activity, job postings, funding, news) and filter signal from noise with AI.

column5: Sales rep lost a major deal because battlecard didn't reflect a new competitor integration. 65% of sales content goes unused.
column8: MiAmar's event intelligence catches competitor product launches and integrations in real-time from LinkedIn and news monitoring. Battlecards auto-update to reflect new moves. Slack integration ensures reps see updates without searching.
column9: CRM opportunity-linked alerts (when a deal involves a specific competitor, auto-surface the latest intel for that competitor directly in the deal view)
column10: The real cost of stale intel isn't the PMM's time — it's the lost deal. When a rep walks into a call not knowing the competitor just launched an integration, they lose credibility instantly. Auto-updating battlecards + real-time event alerts prevent this entirely.

column5: No centralized place for CI. One update in a newsletter, another in a press release, another on their pricing page. Intel is scattered across Slack, Notion, spreadsheets.
column8: MiAmar IS the central source of truth — one dashboard with market overview, event feed, competitor cards, battlecards, prospect cards, and product tracking. All in one workspace, all auto-updated.
column9: Ability to manually add intel snippets from sales calls or customer conversations (field intelligence) alongside the automated monitoring data
column10: The #1 reason CI programs fail is fragmentation. A Slack channel here, a Google Doc there, a Notion page nobody maintains. The fix is a single workspace that auto-populates from monitoring data — not another place to manually dump information.

column5: PMMs become data janitors — 78.6% are responsible for CI at their company. Bulk of time spent finding data, not analyzing it.
column8: MiAmar automates the data collection entirely. AI monitors, classifies, and summarizes competitive signals daily. The PMM's role shifts from find the data to decide what to do with it. Weekly newsletter provides pre-analyzed summaries.
column9: AI-generated strategic recommendations (not just here is what happened but here is what this means for your positioning and what you should do about it)
column10: The PMM job description says competitive strategy. The reality is 80% data collection, 20% strategy. Flip that ratio with automated monitoring and AI summarization. Your time should go to positioning decisions, not copying from LinkedIn into a spreadsheet.

column5: Teams miss competitor funding, product launches, partnerships, hiring signals. Find out from LinkedIn posts that went viral, or worse — on a sales call.
column8: MiAmar monitors all these signal types automatically: LinkedIn posts and profiles, news and press, funding rounds, and job postings. AI classifies events by type (product launches, funding, hires, partnerships) with key takeaway extraction.

column5: Once a rep gets burned by stale intel in front of a customer, they stop trusting ALL battlecards permanently. 65% of sales content goes unused.
column8: MiAmar battlecards show Last updated dates and are refreshed daily from live data. The trust problem disappears when reps see current information every time. Recent competitive moves are highlighted at the top.

column10: Trust in CI is binary — once broken, it's nearly impossible to rebuild. The only fix is cards that are ALWAYS current. Self-updating battlecards with visible last updated: today timestamps rebuild trust because reps verify accuracy on every use.

column5: Klue increased workload to 12 hrs/week (was 8 manually). Salesforce integration slow. 40+ card options overwhelmed reps. Reps said easier to just ask you in Slack.
column8: MiAmar's approach: fewer, better cards that auto-update. Slack integration meets reps where they are. AI meeting prep generates focused briefs for specific calls instead of forcing reps to navigate 40+ generic cards.

column5: Manual competitive analysis means combing through websites, analyst commentary, product pages, content libraries and even Reddit threads. Messy, manual, hours of bleary-eyed tab-switching.

When LinkedIn, news, job boards, and company websites are all monitored automatically and AI extracts the key signals, your competitive research session becomes a 10-minute daily scan instead of a 3-hour deep dive.

column5: Single battlecard takes 20-40 hours to build. Maintaining 5 competitors quarterly = 150-300 hours/year — a part-time job.
column8: MiAmar generates battlecards automatically from monitored data. Competitor cards include overview, recent moves, strengths analysis, and your advantages — all AI-generated and continuously updated. Zero manual build time.

column5: 52% of compete programs have no sales executive sponsor. Sellers rate CI-readiness 3.8/10. CI is under-resourced and disconnected from revenue.
column8: MiAmar's weekly newsletter and Slack integration bring CI directly to sales without requiring executive sponsorship. Low friction adoption — no training needed, intel shows up where teams already work.
column9: Executive dashboard / CI program scorecard (show leadership metrics like competitor moves detected this month and reps using intel before calls to build internal sponsorship)
column10: The sponsorship problem is a chicken-and-egg: leadership won't invest in CI because they can't see ROI, and CI can't show ROI without investment. Break the cycle with a low-cost tool that delivers value immediately — then use the results to justify executive buy-in.
